free_text: Gamma-Ray Production Cross Sections in Multiple
Channels for Neutron-Induced Reaction on 48Ti for En
= 1 to 200 MeV
Main reference.
Reference used in superseded
entry #13397.
Conference presentation.
Weapons Neutron Research facility
WNR at the Los Alamos Neutron Science Center LANSCE
Neutrons are produced by bombarding a natural
tungsten target with the 800-MeV pulsed proton beam
from the LANSCE linac. Neutrons with energies ranging
from a few keV to nearly 800 MeV are produced.
Beam-hardening material ~1.5 cm of lead was placed
in the neutron flight path, and charged particles
were swept from the flight line by permanent magnets
3.3 g of TiO2 enriched to
99.81% 48Ti in the form of a 2.4-cm-diam disk
The gamma rays were detected with the GEANIE
spectrometer, located 20.34 m from the neutron
source on the 60-deg right flight path.
The GEANIE spectrometer consisted of 11
planar and fifteen ~20% to 25% HPGe coaxial
detectors. All of the planar detectors and 9 of the
coaxial detectors were equipped with Compton
suppression shields. The detectors were situated at
a distance of 14 cm from the the scattering sample
The detection time of gamma rays
produced by neutrons interacting with the sample was
used to calculate the TOF of the neutrons relative
to the micropulse time, measured by g-flash detection.
Fission chamber with 235,238U foils, located 2 m
upstream from the GEANIE spectrometer. Fission
chamber events were used to determine the neutron flux.
SD: Subents 68 deleted - duplication with
Subent 2.
BP: Consulted with Prof. L.A.Bernstein on
possible duplication with #13997. The
current entry was updated, and #13997 was
deleted. Updated the entry, STATUS, added
subentries 120-122 with #13997 data sets.
No information on sources of uncertainties.
Data sent by author 08/21/2007 in tabular form.
